title: OECD pricing reforms to affect multinationals - The EastAfrican
summary: OECD pricing reforms to affect multinationals The EastAfrican
ai_summary: This article from The EastAfrican discusses the anticipated impact of ongoing OECD pricing reforms on multinational enterprises. It highlights how these global transfer pricing initiatives are expected to reshape tax liabilities and compliance requirements for MNEs operating across various jurisdictions.

title: Section 40(a)(i) Disallowance Deleted Applying DTAA Non-Discrimination Clause: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi deleted the Section 40(a)(i) disallowance by applying the India-Japan DTAA non-discrimination clause and dismissed the Revenue's appeal. The post Section 40(a)(i) Disallowance Deleted Applying DTAA Non-Discrimination Clause: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Delhi ruled to delete a Section 40(a)(i) disallowance, applying the non-discrimination clause of the India-Japan Double Taxation Avoidance Agreement. This decision dismissed the Revenue's appeal, setting a precedent for the treatment of certain cross-border payments under DTAA provisions in India.

title: Section 54 Exemption Remanded; DVO Valuation Reconsideration Directed After Objections: ITAT Jabalpur
summary: ITAT Jabalpur remanded Section 54 exemption and DVO valuation issues, upheld AY 2015-16 taxability, and allowed retrospective benefit of Section 50C proviso. The post Section 54 Exemption Remanded; DVO Valuation Reconsideration Directed After Objections: ITAT Jabalpur appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Jabalpur remanded issues related to Section 54 exemption and Departmental Valuation Officer (DVO) valuation, directing reconsideration after objections. The Tribunal also upheld AY 2015-16 taxability and allowed retrospective benefit of the Section 50C proviso for capital gains.

title: Section 56(2)(viib) Addition Deleted as DCF Valuation Rejection Unsustainable: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi deleted the Section 56(2)(viib) addition after holding rejection of the DCF valuation and addition of the entire share premium unsustainable. The post Section 56(2)(viib) Addition Deleted as DCF Valuation Rejection Unsustainable: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi deleted an addition made under Section 56(2)(viib), commonly known as 'Angel Tax', which was based on the rejection of a Discounted Cash Flow (DCF) valuation. The Tribunal found the tax authority's rejection of the DCF valuation and the resulting addition of the entire share premium to be unsustainable.

title: Transfer Pricing ALP Adjustment Remanded for Fresh FAR Analysis of Comparables: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi remanded ALP adjustment issues for fresh FAR analysis of two comparables and reconsideration of working capital adjustment. The post Transfer Pricing ALP Adjustment Remanded for Fresh FAR Analysis of Comparables: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Delhi remanded issues pertaining to an Arm's Length Principle (ALP) adjustment in a transfer pricing case. The Tribunal directed a fresh Functional, Asset, and Risk (FAR) analysis for two comparables and ordered a reconsideration of the working capital adjustment.

title: Section 37(1) Business Expenditure Allowed Following Consistent Earlier ITAT Rulings: ITAT Pune
summary: ITAT Pune upheld deletion of Section 37(1) disallowances and AMP transfer pricing adjustment, following consistent earlier orders in the assessee’s own case. The post Section 37(1) Business Expenditure Allowed Following Consistent Earlier ITAT Rulings: ITAT Pune appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Pune upheld the deletion of disallowances under Section 37(1) and an Advertising, Marketing, and Promotion (AMP) transfer pricing adjustment. This decision was made based on consistent earlier orders in the assessee’s own case, reinforcing judicial precedent.

title: Consistent Stock Valuation Without Book Rejection Cannot Be Altered: ITAT Kolkata
summary: ITAT Kolkata dismissed the Revenue’s appeals and held that a consistently followed stock valuation method could not be disturbed without rejecting the books. The post Consistent Stock Valuation Without Book Rejection Cannot Be Altered: ITAT Kolkata appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The ITAT Kolkata dismissed the Revenue’s appeals, ruling that a consistently followed stock valuation method adopted by a taxpayer cannot be disturbed. This holds true unless the tax authorities formally reject the taxpayer's books of account.

title: EY talks tax: Recent trends in the research credit (June 23, 2026)
summary: In this episode, panelists review recent research credit-related court decisions and pending cases and discuss how these decisions are influencing tax administration (IRS and state examinations).
ai_summary: Panelists in an EY discussion reviewed recent US court decisions and pending cases concerning the research tax credit (R&D credit). They examined the influence of these rulings on tax administration practices by both the IRS and various state examination bodies.

title: Dentons Tax Update – Singapore: Transfer pricing treatment of stock-based compensation - Dentons
summary: Dentons Tax Update – Singapore: Transfer pricing treatment of stock-based compensation Dentons
ai_summary: This Dentons tax update provides an analysis of the transfer pricing treatment of stock-based compensation in Singapore. It addresses how such costs should be recognized and allocated between related entities under arm's length principles within the jurisdiction.

title: UN Tackles Headwinds in Shaping Tech Transfer Pricing Guidance
summary: UN Tackles Headwinds in Shaping Tech Transfer Pricing Guidance news.bloombergtax.com
ai_summary: The United Nations is reportedly encountering challenges in its ongoing efforts to develop comprehensive transfer pricing guidance specifically tailored for the technology sector. This initiative aims to provide clarity on applying arm's length principles to the unique business models and value drivers of the digital economy.

title: Confused about UAE corporate tax? FTA answers 15 common business questions - Gulf News
summary: Confused about UAE corporate tax? FTA answers 15 common business questions Gulf News

title: Section 147 Reassessment Quashed as No Income Escaped Assessment: ITAT Agra
summary: ITAT Agra quashed the Section 147 reassessment, holding the amount alleged to have escaped assessment was already included in the assessee's sale proceeds. The post Section 147 Reassessment Quashed as No Income Escaped Assessment: ITAT Agra appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Agra quashed a reassessment initiated under Section 147, determining that no income had escaped assessment. The Tribunal found that the amount alleged to have escaped taxation was already included in the assessee's reported sale proceeds.

title: Routine Support Services Not Royalty Under DTAA; Taxable as Business Profits Without PE: ITAT Delhi
summary: ITAT Delhi held routine support and HR services are not royalty under the India-Philippines and India-Thailand DTAAs. Without a PE, Article 7 bars taxation. The post Routine Support Services Not Royalty Under DTAA; Taxable as Business Profits Without PE: ITAT Delhi appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Delhi ruled that routine support and HR services do not qualify as royalties under the India-Philippines and India-Thailand Double Taxation Avoidance Agreements (DTAAs). Consequently, without a Permanent Establishment (PE) in India, these services are taxable only as business profits in the recipient's country, as per Article 7 of the respective DTAAs.

title: EY talks tax: International tax talk quarterly series with the EY Global Tax Desk Network (June 17, 2026)
summary: Globally integrated businesses continue to drive internationally mobile workforces, with large numbers of personnel now required — or requesting — to work flexibly across jurisdictions or to relocate permanently to different regions. Businesses and tax functions must meet that operational demand to manage and retain talent, while remaining sufficiently flexible to adapt to global events. In this episode, panelists discuss trends in short- and long-term mobility in the context of evolving personal tax regimes.
ai_summary: This EY international tax talk, part of a quarterly series, delves into trends concerning internationally mobile workforces and evolving personal tax regimes. The discussion addresses how global businesses manage talent and adapt to operational demands for flexible work arrangements across jurisdictions.

title: Mumbai ITAT Deletes Section 69A Addition as CHA Customs Duty Collections Are Fiduciary Receipts
summary: ITAT Mumbai held that customs duty collections received by a CHA in a fiduciary capacity are not taxable under Section 69A and deleted the ₹1.36 crore addition. title: Transfer Pricing Documentation Checklist for FY 2025-26: Key Compliance Requirements
summary: Practical guide to FY 2025-26 transfer pricing compliance covering Section 92D, Rule 10D, Form 3CEB, Master File, Local File, CbCR and APA. The post Transfer Pricing Documentation Checklist for FY 2025-26: Key Compliance Requirements appeared first on TaxGuru .
ai_summary: This guide provides a practical checklist for Indian transfer pricing compliance for FY 2025-26, detailing key requirements under Section 92D and Rule 10D. It covers the preparation of Form 3CEB, Master File, Local File, CbCR, and considerations for Advance Pricing Agreements.

title: ITAT Delhi Allows Paper Company's Appeal In Captive Power Transfer Pricing Dispute - LiveLawBiz

ai_summary: The Income Tax Appellate Tribunal (ITAT) Delhi has sided with a paper company in its appeal regarding a transfer pricing dispute related to captive power transactions. This ruling from a significant Indian tribunal offers important insights into the valuation of intra-group services and cost allocations within India's transfer pricing framework.

title: Coca-Cola’s $9-bn US tax appeal may shape India’s transfer pricing rules - MSN
summary: Coca-Cola’s $9-bn US tax appeal may shape India’s transfer pricing rules MSN
ai_summary: Coca-Cola's substantial $9 billion US tax appeal, centered on intricate transfer pricing adjustments and the allocation of profits related to intangibles, could significantly influence India's future transfer pricing rules. The outcome of this high-profile US case is being closely watched for its potential precedents and wider implications for global tax administrations.
